steps.title: Find the slant height of each pyramid
steps.content: Both bases are 100 cm², so the base side \(s=10\) cm. Slant height uses half the base (5 cm) and pyramid height \(h\): $$l=\sqrt{h^2+5^2}$$ Pyramid 1 (\(h=11\)): \(l_1=\sqrt{121+25}=\sqrt{146}\approx12.08\) cm. Pyramid 2 (\(h=22\)): \(l_2=\sqrt{484+25}=\sqrt{509}\approx22.56\) cm.

steps.title: Compute lateral surface areas and their difference
steps.content: Each pyramid has 4 triangular faces with area \(\tfrac{1}{2}\cdot10\cdot l=5l\). Lateral area difference: $$4\cdot5(l_2-l_1)=20(\sqrt{509}-\sqrt{146})\approx20(22.56-12.08)\approx20(10.48)\approx209.6\text{ cm}^2$$ The bases are equal so they cancel.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: 209 — closest to 209.6. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: 220 — off by about 10. Incorrect.
  • C: 367 — too large. Incorrect.
  • D: 551 — this would be the full surface area of pyramid 2, not the difference. Incorrect.
